commit.message:
Rewrite: Verlet cloth physics with tearing, 5 force modes, shockwaves

- Full Verlet integration cloth mesh (~2000 nodes, ~7000 springs)
- Springs break under tension with elastic impulse snap
- Disconnected nodes detach and become free particles
- 5 genuinely different force modes (scroll to switch):
  GRAVITY  — Newtonian gravity well, orbits and slingshots
  VORTEX   — Tangential spiral + turbulent curl noise
  REPULSE  — Explosive radial repulsion
  WARP     — Time-varying Clifford attractor field
  ACID     — Random jitter + probabilistic spring dissolution
- Click = shockwave that tears mesh and launches nodes
- Screen shake on impact
- 3-pass spring rendering: batched low-stress, batched medium,
  individual glow for critical springs (red/white at breaking)
- Velocity streaks on fast-moving nodes
- R or double-click to rebuild mesh
- Touch support: drag, tap, two-finger = mode shift

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent deleted toolbar bookmarks from being reverted by auto-sync (#6)

* fix: prevent deleted toolbar bookmarks from being reverted by auto-sync

Race condition: when a user deletes a bookmark while a sync is running,
performSync's storeTombstones() overwrites the tombstone created by the
onRemoved listener, causing the follow-up sync to re-add the bookmark
from cloud.

Fix: re-read current tombstones from storage before writing to preserve
any tombstones added concurrently during the sync.

* test: add integration test calling real performSync with mocked browser APIs

Add __test__ exports (VITEST-only, tree-shaken in production) to
background/index.js so integration tests can import and call the real
performSync, addTombstone, categorizeCloudBookmarks, etc.

New test file: toolbar-delete-race-integration.test.js (10 tests)
- Calls the REAL performSync with fully mocked browser.* and fetch APIs
- Simulates the tombstone race condition: delays cloud GET, fires
  onRemoved mid-sync, verifies tombstone is preserved after sync
- Verifies deleted bookmark is NOT re-added from cloud
- Tests tombstone merge, categorization filtering, concurrent sync
  guard, and state management
- All 10 tests pass; 527 total passing (was 517)

* Update apps/extension/__tests__/toolbar-delete-revert.test.js

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: add screenshots/ to .gitignore

* chore(release): v0.8.27

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(antipattern): detect complex init() functions violating Go best practices

- Add checkInitFunctionComplexity() to detect init() functions with high cyclomatic complexity
- Flag init functions with complexity > 5 as medium-severity issues
- Add helper methods isInitFunction() and calculateCyclomaticComplexity()
- Initialize PerformanceAntipatterns field in AntiPatternMetrics
- Improve code alignment in createInitialAntiPatterns()

Complex init() functions are difficult to test and understand, making them a
common pattern in LLM-generated code. This detection helps enforce Go best
practices for initialization logic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ac): mark session completed when cycle gated — prevent watchdog resume loop

When an AC cycle is gated/failed, mark the session as 'completed' so
the stuck-session watchdog doesn't pick it up and resume it, which
caused an infinite VETO gate loop.

Fix cloud model run flow: use proper model suffixes (#21)

* fix: fetch cloud models with proper suffixes from library

Cloud models have varying suffixes (e.g., :671b-cloud, :480b-cloud) that must be included when starting/stopping them. Now fetching from library with 'cloud' tag to get full model names and matching with cloud API status.

- Fetch cloud models from library using tag=cloud to get full names with suffixes
- Match library names with cloud API status to determine running/stopped state
- Remove library integration (cloud models stay in cloud pane only)
- Remove auto-pull logic since proper names are now used
- Simplify LibraryModelsProvider constructor
- Update all tests to match new signatures
- All 203 tests passing

* fix: normalize model family tree and cloud tag resolution

- remove library recency sort commands and setting

- add family grouping across local/library/cloud trees

- normalize family prefixes (e.g. qwen3* -> qwen)

- collapse redundant singleton library parent nodes

- resolve cloud run names to :cloud or :-cloud variants

- update tests for grouped hierarchy and cloud resolution

* fix: auto-pull missing cloud models before start

Retry start after pulling when cloud-tagged model is not found

Covers :cloud and :-cloud tags (e.g. kimi-k2-thinking:cloud)

* fix: refine cloud warm-state UX and family grouping edge cases

- optimistic cloud running/stopped icon toggles on start/stop

- pull cloud-tagged models before start and improve readiness messaging

- hide pulled cloud-tagged models from local model list

- group qwen2.5vl-style names under qwen family

* Update README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(api): implement missing monitoring and network status endpoints (#1801) (#1803)

* feat(api): implement missing monitoring and network status endpoints (#1801)

Add missing API endpoints for monitoring and network status:

**Network Endpoints:**
- GET /api/v1/network/status - Network connectivity and sync status
- GET /api/v1/network/peers - Connected peers list (shorter path)

**Monitoring Endpoints:**
- GET /api/v1/monitor/health - Component health checks
- GET /api/v1/monitor/system - System metrics (CPU, memory, disk)
- GET /api/v1/monitor/performance - Blockchain performance metrics

**Changes:**
- Added handle_get_network_status() to NetworkHandler
- Created new MonitorHandler with health/system/performance endpoints
- Registered MonitorHandler in unified_server.rs
- Added monitor module to api/handlers/mod.rs

All endpoints return properly structured JSON responses as specified
in the issue. Placeholder values are used for system metrics until
full system monitoring is implemented.

Closes #1801

* Update zhtp/src/api/handlers/monitor.rs

commit.message:
fix: two issue cache bugs — stale data and case mismatch

Bug 1: Background refresh used loadSprint() which checks saved state
first and returns early — external GitHub changes (closing issues)
were never picked up. Fix: refreshAll() now calls refreshFromGitHub()
which always queries GitHub, bypassing saved state.

Bug 2: Two more locations in ws-server.ts compared i.state === 'closed'
(lowercase) against gh CLI's 'CLOSED' (uppercase). Both handleSprintIssues
cache-miss path and loadHistoricalIssues had the same case mismatch.
Fix: normalize with .toLowerCase() in all three locations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add Redis-backed tests for RedisDeviceAuthService using Moq'd IConnectionMultiplexer/IDatabase
  - Verify Lua script receives correct keys for atomic rate limiting
  - Verify device registration and TTL refresh via Redis batches
  - Verify max device count enforcement
- Add Redis-backed tests for RedisStateStoreService
  - Verify StringSetAsync called with correct key prefix and TTL
  - Verify atomic StringGetDeleteAsync for state consumption
  - Test custom expiry passthrough
- Add Redis exception fallback tests for both services
  - Verify graceful degradation to in-memory when Redis throws
  - Verify rate limits still enforced in fallback mode
- Add rate limit enforcement tests (hourly limit = 100)
  - Verify 101st request is rejected
  - Verify different device tokens have independent rate limit buckets
  - Verify multiple over-limit attempts all rejected
- Add state validation tests for YouTube and Spotify OAuth Initiate
  - Capture state via Moq Callback, verify it matches response body and auth URL
- Add missing Spotify rate limit tests for Exchange and Refresh endpoints
- Add Spotify missing device token test for Refresh
- Add Apple Music developer token caching test

- Add StackExchange.Redis 2.9.32 to test project for Redis type mocking

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: harden URL validation against SSRF, OAuth bypass, and edge-case attacks

Security vulnerabilities fixed in validation.cjs:
- isAllowedAIHost: reject userinfo credential-smuggling and non-standard ports
- isValidRedirectUri: replace startsWith with URL parsing for djai:// protocol
  (fixes prefix-matching bypass: djai://oauth/callback.evil, path traversal)
- isValidRedirectUri: enforce /oauth/callback path for localhost redirects
- isTTSResponseWithinLimit: reject negative, NaN, and Infinity values
- isAllowedOAuthHost: reject userinfo and non-standard ports

Security vulnerabilities fixed in main.cjs:
- Replace url.startsWith(redirectUri) with isOAuthCallback() in will-redirect
  and will-navigate handlers (fixes callbackevil.com prefix bypass)
- Replace startsWith in handleDeepLink with isDjaiOAuthCallback()
- Replace startsWith in yt-music-play-url with isValidYouTubeMusicUrl()
- Use isAllowedExternalProtocol() in setWindowOpenHandler

New extracted+tested functions in validation.cjs:
- isOAuthCallback(url, redirectUri) — proper URL component matching
- isDjaiOAuthCallback(url) — djai:// deep-link validation
- isAllowedExternalProtocol(url) — protocol allowlist for shell.openExternal
- isValidYouTubeMusicUrl(url) — YouTube Music hostname validation

Test coverage: 49 → 100 tests (all passing), covering:
- SSRF: path-based, userinfo, encoded-dot, non-standard port bypasses
- OAuth: prefix-matching, path traversal, userinfo hijack attacks
- djai:// protocol: prefix bypass, path traversal
- TTS: negative, NaN, Infinity edge cases
- CSP: unsafe-eval absence, unsafe-inline scoping, wildcard checks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Frontend (414 tests via Vitest):
- Unit tests: TTS services (5 providers), AI commentary, music providers

Infrastructure:

- Feedback loop: test-summary.ps1 aggregates results, test:failures for agents
- JSON/JUnit/TRX reporters for CI consumption

Bugs found and fixed by honest tests:

- main.cjs: OAuth callback startsWith prefix bypass (5 security vulns)

rate limit enforcement, silent-pass assertions, security bypass gaps)

Each file includes concept explanations, DJ.ai source references,
links to official documentation, code examples, and key takeaways.

commit.message:
Fix crash: update before filter to prevent negative canvas radii

filter → update → draw caused life to go negative during draw(),
passing negative radius to createRadialGradient/arc which throws
an IndexSizeError and kills the animation loop.

Fixed order: update → filter → draw.

commit.message:
v0.1.41: P2P tile retry fix - shared failed-tile tracking across all fetcher instances

- Move failed_elev/failed_osm HashSets into TileFetcher (Arc<Mutex<...>>)
  so all three P2PElevationSource instances share the same knowledge
- When a tile is missing from all peers, mark it failed once — no 3x repeat requests
- When a new peer connects, clear failed sets — new peer may have missing tiles
- When a tile is announced (available), clear its failed entry
- Remove per-instance failed_tiles from P2PElevationSource
- Apply same pattern to OSM tile fetching (fetch_osm)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(security): harden platform against arXiv:2602.20021 vulnerabilities

11 vulnerability classes addressed from red-teaming study of LLM agents:

## Adversarial guard (adversarial.py)
- PROMPT_INJECTION L0 (+8): detect instruction override in output/tool results [SBD-09]
- IDENTITY_CLAIM L0 (+7): detect agent impersonation ('I am Jarvis/admin/platform') [SBD-06]
- RESOURCE_ABUSE L0 (+7): detect DoS patterns in written code (busy-loops, fork bombs) [SBD-04]
- HARDCODED_SECRET: extend with JWT/Azure SAS/OpenAI key patterns [SBD-02]
- SECURITY_VULN: eval/exec/pickle/sql-injection/shell=True [SBD-08]
- FALSE_FALLBACK: stub/TODO detection in production code

## Tool scope (code_tools.py)
- _SENSITIVE_FILE_RE: block read/write/edit of .env, *.key, *.pem, SSH keys [SBD-02/SBD-08]
- audit_log on code_write (wires existing security/audit.py) [SBD-08]

## A2A bus (bus.py)
- from_agent identity validation: log+flag unregistered senders [SBD-06]
- Keeps existing _check_delegation_scope for SBD-01

## Memory sanitization (memory/manager.py)
- project_store + global_store: sanitize_agent_output() before INSERT [SBD-10/SBD-11]
- max 8K value cap to prevent memory DoS

## Executor budget (executor.py)
- MAX_TOOL_CALLS_PER_RUN=50 (env override) per-run hard limit [SBD-05]
- Raises BudgetExceededError on breach

## Documentation (CLAUDE.md)
- SECURITY section with arXiv:2602.20021 reference, threat table, Swiss Cheese model,
  intentional non-mitigations (Landlock, RBAC, A2A hard-block)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add Prisma schema for all domain models

- Add HTTP contracts for training API
- Add Docker Compose with PostgreSQL and API service
- Add Jest configuration with 12 passing unit tests for training domain

commit.message:
fix: add log.error() to 65 silent catch blocks in routes.ts

Every catch block returning res.status(500) now logs the error
server-side via Pino before responding to the client. Uses the
pattern: log.error({ err: e }, "<METHOD> <path> failed").

Works directly with Azure Blob Storage registry.

refactor(api): create buildOperationContext helper — deduplicate 63 routes (E-04)

userAgent, endpoint, method) into a shared buildOperationContext() helper.

Updated all 63 route files (167+ call sites). Each context object replaced

commit.message:
Fix Windows build and restructure CI workflows

- Replace Unix-only syscall.Flock with cross-platform github.com/gofrs/flock
  (fixes Windows build failure)
- Simplify CI build job to single compilation check (no matrix)
- Move cross-platform binary builds to new build.yml workflow
  triggered by version tags (v*) or manual workflow_dispatch
- goreleaser releases unaffected (release.yml unchanged)

commit.message:
feat(hooks): instinct system + ECC module + skill-stocktake + agent hook assignments

- platform/hooks/instinct.py: InstinctObserver adapted from ECC continuous-learning-v2
  - analyze_session(): 3 pattern types (bigram, dominant workflow, read-before-write)
  - upsert_instinct(): confidence tiers (0.3→0.9) with boosting on re-observation
  - evolve_instincts(): cluster by domain → skill YAML generation
  - observe_session(): async entry point fired from SESSION_END hook

- platform/hooks/builtins.py: 7th handler _instinct_observer + ECC source docstring

- platform/hooks/__init__.py: ECC traceability docstring (adaptation rationale)

- platform/web/routes/api/instincts.py: 6 endpoints
  GET  /api/instincts, GET /api/instincts/stats, POST /api/instincts
  DELETE /api/instincts/{id}, POST /api/instincts/evolve, GET /api/skills/stocktake

- platform/db/migrations.py:
  - Tables: instinct_observations, instincts (TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW() for PG compat)
  - ECC external module seeded in integrations table (Settings > Modules)
  - 6 agent-specific hook_registrations seeds

SOURCE: https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code
WHY: Instincts = atomic learned behaviors extracted from sessions -> skill evolution.

commit.message:
feat: add x402 seller HTTP server with paid API endpoints

Add an optional Express HTTP server that exposes MCP tools as x402
payment-gated endpoints. Other AI agents and clients can pay USDC to
query wallet data through standardized HTTP endpoints.

Endpoints:
  GET /api/usdc-balance       (0.001 USDC)
  GET /api/sol-balance        (0.001 USDC)
  GET /api/wallet-info        (0.001 USDC)
  GET /api/incoming-payments  (0.01 USDC)

New files:
  src/server.ts — Express app factory with @x402/express middleware

Config changes:
  X402_SERVER_PORT — opt-in: server only starts when this is set
  X402_FACILITATOR_URL — defaults to https://x402.org/facilitator

The MCP client is shared between the CLI REPL and HTTP server.
Server shuts down gracefully on SIGINT/SIGTERM alongside the CLI.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
